title: DELETE /api/user/me — delete account and all data
description: Implement the endpoint to permanently delete the current user's account and all associated weight entries.
acceptanceCriteria: User record is removed from the database
All weight entries for that user are also deleted (cascade)
The deleted user's JWT is no longer valid after deletion
Returns 401 if no valid JWT provided

steps.details: In user router, add DELETE /me route with response_code=204. Handler: db.delete(current_user), db.commit(). Weight entries are deleted via cascade (defined on the FK). Return Response(status_code=204).

steps.description: Enable SQLite foreign key cascade enforcement
steps.details: In src/api/database.py, add an event listener to enable foreign keys on each connection: from sqlalchemy import event; @event.listens_for(engine, 'connect') def set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_connection, connection_record): cursor = dbapi_connection.cursor(); cursor.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON'); cursor.close(). This is required for CASCADE DELETE to work in SQLite.

Create a user, add 3 weight entries, then DELETE /api/user/me with their JWT. Verify: status 204. Then GET /api/auth/me with the same token — verify 401. Query the database and verify no weight_entries exist for that user_id.

technicalNotes: SQLite requires PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON per connection for cascade deletes to work — this is a common SQLite gotcha
After deletion, the JWT still contains a valid user_id but the user no longer exists — GET /api/auth/me will return 401 because the user lookup fails
